templates.name: haiku
templates.whenToUse: Your first workflow — learn agent() and structured (zod) output in one screen.

templates.name: pr-review
templates.description: Review a diff, flag bugs and risks, suggest fixes
templates.whenToUse: When you want a structured second opinion on a PR before merge.

templates.name: test-backfill
templates.description: Generate missing tests for a target module
templates.whenToUse: When a module is under-tested and you want coverage filled in test-first.

templates.name: doc-gen
templates.description: Generate/refresh a README section or docstrings
templates.whenToUse: When docs have drifted from the code and you want a fresh, accurate section.

templates.name: codebase-migration
templates.whenToUse: When a rename or API migration spans many files and you want it done in parallel.

templates.name: dependency-upgrade
templates.description: Bump a dep, fix breakages, summarize changelog risk
templates.whenToUse: When upgrading a dependency and you want breakages fixed and the risk summarized.

templates.name: incident-triage
templates.description: Triage a stack trace; asks you which lead to pursue
templates.whenToUse: When you have a stack trace and want hypotheses, then to steer which one to chase.

templates.name: multi-file-haiku
templates.description: Folder layout: slim entry + schemas.ts + prompts.ts
templates.whenToUse: When you want the canonical folder layout for a workflow split across files.
